mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and consumption capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or unemployment spikes fail to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts yield positive forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term corporate earnings strength and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with industrial production changes, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation decelerates while the federal funds rate remains near zero, market participants price in sustained monetary accommodation and reduced input cost pressures, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or deflationary risk regime
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in more than 55% of low-inflation, low-rate periods, or the mechanism reverses direction during inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in total nonfarm payrolls combined with a rising unemployment rate confirms a structural labor market deterioration, which reduces aggregate demand forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 200000 in a single month
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms an economic contraction, which forces downward revisions to corporate earnings models and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of negative-GDP quarters, or GDP contraction fails to predict return direction with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stabilizing housing starts signals a broad-based supply-side recovery, which improves equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined supply signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mechanism breaks down during demand-led downturns with no predictive power.
